perturbation.original_text: B. In consideration for the services to be provided herein, MKOS agrees that it will remit the agreed upon stock certificate or cash payment within five (5) days of both parties executing this Agreement. If no consideration is received in the timeline, UTEK has the unilateral option to terminate this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: B. In consideration for the services to be provided herein, MKOS agrees that it will remit the agreed upon stock certificate or cash payment within fifteen (15) days of both parties executing this Agreement. If no consideration is received in the timeline, UTEK has the unilateral option to terminate this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by modifying the payment deadline from five (5) days to fifteen (15) days after the execution of the agreement. This directly contradicts section IV.A, which implies a monthly vesting schedule tied to service delivery, thus making uncertain as to when payment should begin. The new term creates uncertainty around UTEK's termination rights based on payment receipt timelines.
perturbation.contradicted_text: A. In consideration for providing these Services, MKOS shall pay UTEK $120,000 in the form of unregistered shares of common stock (923,077 shares) upon the execution of this Strategic Alliance Agreement. 1/12th of the shares (76,923) shall vest each month during the term of this Agreement. In lieu of payment of shares, MKOS shall have the option of paying UTEK $10,000 per month for the Services described in this Agreement. If this Agreement is terminated any unvested shares will be returned to MKOS.
perturbation.justification: The change in the payment deadline from five (5) days to fifteen (15) days directly contradicts the original payment schedule in Section A, which specifies that payment should occur upon the execution of the agreement and further specifies a vesting schedule, or in lieu of the stock payment, then payments of $10,000 per month. The changed text introduces an inconsistency regarding when payment should be expected, potentially affecting UTEK's termination rights and creating uncertainty about the payment timeline, which is covered explicitly in both sections. The introduction of 15 days creates an inconsistency and introduces ambiguity when the contradicted clause expects certainty.

perturbation.original_text: Either party may terminate this Agreement at any time with 30 days written notice.
perturbation.changed_text: Either party may terminate this Agreement at any time with 15 days written notice.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction by changing the written notice period required for termination from 30 days to 15 days. This directly clashes with section VIII.C related to the twenty-four month non-solicitation agreement following the execution of this agreement, where termination based on the modified 15 day written notice impacts that section, since either party could now terminate with 15 days notice, and the non-solicitation agreement remains at twenty-four months. The modified time creates uncertainty in the period and obligation from VIII.C.
perturbation.contradicted_text: C. SOLICITATION. MKOS agrees that for a twenty four months (24) following the execution of this Agreement, MKOS shall not, without UTEK's prior written consent, directly or indirectly solicit for employment any present employee of UTEK, or request, induce or advise any employee of UTEK to leave the employ of UTEK. In turn, UTEK agrees that it will not directly or indirectly solicit any present employee of MKOS.
perturbation.justification: The changed text reduces the termination notice period from 30 days to 15 days. This directly contradicts section VIII.C, which stipulates a twenty-four month non-solicitation agreement following the *execution of this Agreement*. Since MKOS could now terminate with 15 days notice, the length of the non-solicitation agreement is impacted, creating an inconsistency regarding the obligations and timeframe laid out in VIII.C.

perturbation.original_text: F. ASSIGNMENTS. The benefits of the Agreement shall inure to the respective successors and assignees of the parties and assigns and representatives, and the obligations and liabilities assumed in this Agreement by the parties hereto shall be binding upon their respective successors and assigns; provided that the rights and obligations of UTEK under this Agreement may not be assigned or delegated without the prior written consent of MKOS and any such purported assignment shall be null and void. Notwithstanding the foregoing, UTEK may assign this Agreement or any portion of its Compensation as outlined herein to its subsidiaries in its sole discretion.
perturbation.changed_text: F. ASSIGNMENTS. The benefits of the Agreement shall inure to the respective successors and assignees of the parties and assigns and representatives, and the obligations and liabilities assumed in this Agreement by the parties hereto shall be binding upon their respective successors and assigns; provided that the rights and obligations of UTEK under this Agreement may not be assigned or delegated without the prior written consent of MKOS, which cannot be unduly withheld, and any such purported assignment shall be null and void. Notwithstanding the foregoing, UTEK may assign this Agreement or any portion of its Compensation as outlined herein to its subsidiaries in its sole discretion, or to any entity it deems fit.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction concerning assignment rights. Firstly, it adds that MKOS's consent for UTEK's assignment cannot be 'unduly withheld', which creates potential disputes on what constitutes 'unduly withheld'. Secondly, it changes the scope of UTEK's assignment rights by including 'or to any entity it deems fit', thus allowing UTEK to assign the agreement to any entity, contradicting the prior requirement for MKOS's approval. This contradicts section VIII.A regarding indemnity, since it is not clear who will indemnify who, if UTEK assigns the contract to an entity it deems fit, without MKOS's consent.
perturbation.contradicted_text: A. INDEMNITY. 1. UTEK shall indemnify, defend and hold harmless MKOS from and against any and all losses incurred by MKOS which arise out of or result from misrepresentation, breach of warranty or breach or non- fulfillment of any covenant contained herein or Schedules annexed hereto or in any other documents or instruments furnished by UTEK pursuant hereto or in connection with this Agreement. 2. MKOS shall indemnify, defend and hold harmless UTEK from and against any and all losses incurred by UTEK which arise out of or result from misrepresentation, breach of warranty or breach or non-fulfillment of any covenant contained herein or Schedules annexed hereto or in any other documents or instruments furnished by MKOS pursuant hereto or in connection with this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text allows UTEK to assign the agreement to any entity it deems fit, removing the requirement for MKOS's prior written consent, which directly contradicts the original assignment clause. This creates a contradiction with Section A of Indemnity because the assignment by UTEK to any entity it deems fit, without MKOS's consent, causes unclarity on who would indemnify who, if UTEK assigns the contract to an entity it deems fit, without MKOS's consent. The original clause stated UTEK shall indemnify, defend and hold harmless MKOS, but if the contract is assigned, it is unclear whether UTEK remains responsible or the assignee.
